title: Intensely Smoky Croatian Bulgur Wheat Pilaf
description: A traditional Croatian dish featuring fermented and smoky bulgur wheat, perfect for a hangover cure, this pilaf is crumbly and full of flavor, made without onions or garlic for a friendly twist.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the fermented bulgur wheat and drain well.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Add the smoked paprika, caraway seeds, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the bulgur wheat to the skillet and stir to coat with the spice mixture.
Add the vegetable broth and liquid smoke to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the oven and stir in the chopped parsley.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
